ogTitle: Gunning Gap vs Gunningbland: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gunning Gap and Gunningbland.
heading: Gunning Gap vs Gunningbland — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gunning Gap, New South Wales and Gunningbland, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gunning Gap has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Gunningbland (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gunning Gap or Gunningbland safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gunning Gap has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Gunningbland scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gunning Gap scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Gunningbland scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gunning Gap or Gunningbland?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gunning Gap has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
